summary: The case shows an idiographic pattern of early-morning awakening with rapid cognitive arousal, active doomscrolling, and daytime impairment. Modifiable BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers targeting arousal regulation, screen/BED behaviors, circadian entrainment, and environmental factors are proposed. Each lever is atomic, measurable, and feasible to implement, with explicit mapping to the outcome criteria (C01–C06) and practical time horizons.

solutions.predictor_variable: No nocturnal device engagement during awakenings; implement a brief, non-screen rest protocol and limit doomscrolling in the 60-90 minute wake window
solutions.solution_candidate: During any awakening around 04:30, enforce a 60–90 minute wake window where the person avoids screens and engages in a passive-rest alternative (e.g., 2–3 minutes of slow diaphragmatic breathing or a brief body scan, followed by journaling or a reflective cue to shift thoughts). Remove easy access to doomscroll content (phone out of reach, use grayscale/blackout mode if needed). Goal is to reduce immediate arousal (C03) and interrupt stimulant doomscrolling (C04, C05).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By replacing screen-driven arousal with brief, calming, non-stimulating activities, physiological arousal decreases and cognitive hyperactivation is interrupted, reducing time awake at the 04:30 wake and limiting nocturnal rumination that fuels daytime fatigue (C06).
solutions.measurement: Average minutes of doomscrolling during nocturnal awakenings; self-reported time spent awake in bed after awakenings; frequency/duration of device use in the bed during awakenings.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns identified; monitor for increased anxiety if journal prompts elicit distress. If new or worsening suicidality or distress occurs, seek professional help.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S01 to C03 (arousal), C04 (in-bed device engagement), and C05 (doomscrolling exposure); aiming to reduce arousal and screen-based activation during nocturnal awakenings.

solutions.predictor_variable: Immediate arousal management upon waking (04:30 episodes) with a brief calming routine
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a 3-minute boxed breathing exercise followed by a 1–2 minute grounding or cognitive reframe (e.g., naming 3 things you can control) immediately upon awakening. If sleep pressure allows, return to bed only after the routine; otherwise shift to low-stimulation activity in a dim environment. This targets C03 and C04/C05 by dampening intrusions and reducing the urge to doomscroll.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: A quick, evidence-informed arousal regulation routine reduces physiological and cognitive activation, improving the ability to either resume sleep with lower arousal or transition to a calm wake state, thereby decreasing daytime fatigue (C06).
solutions.measurement: Frequency of immediate arousal management use; self-rated arousal level within 5 minutes of waking (0–1); minutes of doomscrolling in the following 60 minutes.
solutions.safety_notes: If the routine triggers distress or panic, discontinue and seek clinician guidance.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S02 to C03 (arousal) and C04/C05 (screen use after awakening); evaluate arousal reduction and engagement with doomscrolling.

solutions.predictor_variable: Morning circadian entrainment via targeted daytime light exposure
solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce 15–30 minutes of bright light exposure (natural daylight or a light-therapy device) soon after awakening (within 20–60 minutes) to stabilize circadian rhythm, promote alertness without prolonging wakefulness in the middle of the night, and reduce preference for doomscrolling.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Bright light after waking reinforces circadian cues and supports daytime alertness, reducing premature awakenings and hyperarousal, which indirectly lowers nightly disruption and daytime fatigue (C01, C03).
solutions.measurement: Duration of morning light exposure; subjective sleep maintenance rating; perceived daytime energy by lunch (0–1 scale).
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure light exposure is non-distracting and avoid excessive light intensity for sensitive individuals; consult if any photophobia or eye discomfort arises.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S03 to C01 (wake time consistency) and C03 (arousal) via circadian entrainment effects.

solutions.predictor_variable: CBT-I micro-skills delivery (stimulus control + sleep restriction adaptations)
solutions.solution_candidate: Provide a compact CBT-I module or coaching focusing on sleep efficiency, consistent bed and wake times, and re-association of bed with sleep only. Include a simple sleep window, use of a pre-sleep routine, and urge-resistance to doomscrolling. This directly targets C01 and C04, with downstream effects on C03 and C05.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured CBT-I components improve sleep onset, maintenance, and reduce cognitive rumination and screen-based arousal, leading to more consolidated sleep and better daytime function.
solutions.measurement: Bedtime/wake-time consistency (minutes variance); total time in bed vs. sleep time; self-reported arousal and doomscrolling episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: If sleep restriction worsens daytime functioning, adjust plan or consult clinician.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S04 to C01 (sleep maintenance), C04 (in-bed behaviors), and indirectly to C03/C05 via arousal reduction.

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening digital content management and screen-curfew
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a 60–90 minute digital curfew before bedtime, enable blue-light reduction settings, and replace doomscrolling with non-content-related wind-down activities (e.g., reading, stretching). This targets late-evening arousal and reduces late-night cognitive activation (C05) and improvised in-bed engagement (C04).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing late-evening screen exposure reduces cognitive arousal and negative affective activation that feed into early-morning wakefulness and daytime fatigue.
solutions.measurement: Window of last screen use before bed; minutes of doomscrolling after bedtime; self-reported sleep quality and wakefulness on following day.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for adherence; if social or occupational demands require late screen use, adapt plan.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S05 to C05 (doomscrolling) and C04 (in-bed device engagement); evaluate impact on nighttime arousal.

solutions.solution_candidate: Enhance the sleep environment with blackout curtains, cooler room temperature (18–20°C), white-noise or fan, and minimized light/visual distractions. This reduces nocturnal sensory arousal and supports sleep maintenance (C01) and daytime functioning (C06).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: An optimized environment lowers physiological arousal and facilitates easier sleep maintenance, reducing wakefulness after sleep onset and improving daytime energy.
solutions.measurement: Room temperature, darkness quality (light leakage), presence of white-noise; self-reported sleep continuity; energy levels by lunchtime.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; ensure environmental changes are comfortable and adjustable.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S06 to C01 (sleep maintenance) and C06 (midday functioning) via environmental stabilization.
